title: Pseudoglossanthis tianschanica

It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, which include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pseudoglossanthis tianshanica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pseudoglossanthis tianschanica is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and can also be found in the form of capsules, tablets, or dried extracts.

Pseudoglossanthis tianschanica is also known to be a perennial herb that grows in mountainous regions.
